endpoints.name: Crash and Burn (DFT-119) — Goblin Rocketeers flag
Back on Track (DFT-76) — Champions of Amonkhet flag
Aether Rangers (existing) — considered as absorbing anchor
Endriders (existing) — sibling team for cohort precedent

refusal_rationale.aether_rangers_absorbs_none_of_the_three:
Aether Rangers is canonically the Avishkar home team — Pia Nalaar / Sita Varma / Avishkar Assembly-funded, the post-Indigo-Revolution cross-class comrade team. The three slate cards each name a DIFFERENT team:
  - Crash and Burn's flavor names Redshift (Goblin Rocketeers co-captain with Daretti)
  - Trade the Helm's art depicts a Keelhauler boarding a Rocketeer / Endrider vehicle (Keelhaulers are Kari Zev / Howler's aquatic-humanoid team)
  - Back on Track's flavor names the Tombs Before Time / Amonkhet (Champions of Amonkhet team, Basri Ket + Zahur co-captains)
None of the three teams overlap with the Aether Rangers' Avishkar-home-team identity. Absorbing any of them into aether-rangers.md would be an Edgelord-grade graph error: cross-team conflation at the character-layer.
refusal_rationale.endriders_absorbs_none_either: Endriders are Mardu-leaning Gastal apocalypse-punk; same set, different team. Same refusal logic applies.
refusal_rationale.why_not_three_new_team_nodes_this_pass: Per parent-orchestrator discipline: one-per-invocation rule, no new structural-primitive vocabulary, and Prong B already absorbed the Mr. Nodeley budget (refused). Commissioning three new team character-nodes in one pass would violate the one-per-invocation rule three times over. Furthermore, the slate-density isn't yet at threshold-of-3-anchors per team in our corpus: Goblin Rocketeers has 1 trivia-attested card (Crash and Burn), Keelhaulers has 1 (Trade the Helm), Champions of Amonkhet has 1 (Back on Track). The threshold-of-3 precedent ([[trunks]] / [[pan]] / [[aether-rangers]] / [[endriders]]) requires 3+ corpus anchors per team before solo-team-node commissioning.

triage_candidates_surfaced.future_trigger: When 2 more Goblin Rocketeers trivia-attested DFT cards land (Daretti, Rocketeer Engineer DFT-120 is corpus-absent but canonical; other red/green DFT pit-crew cards may exist), evaluate node commissioning. Sibling pattern: aether-rangers, endriders.
triage_candidates_surfaced.canonical_basis: magic.wizards.com: The Legends of Aetherdrift names Redshift + Daretti as co-captains; MTG Wiki has a Goblin Rocketeers team page per Aetherdrift coverage.

triage_candidates_surfaced.future_trigger: When 2 more Keelhauler-attested DFT cards land (Kari Zev, Howler — both canonical co-captains, corpus-absent), evaluate node commissioning. Same sibling pattern.
triage_candidates_surfaced.canonical_basis: magic.wizards.com: All 10 Racing Teams page; the team is documented in DFT Planeswalker's Guide coverage.

triage_candidates_surfaced.future_trigger: When 2 more Champions of Amonkhet-attested DFT cards land (Basri Ket DFT-anchored print, Zahur Eternal Charioteer DFT, additional Amonkhet-flavored DFT cards), evaluate node commissioning. Same sibling pattern.
triage_candidates_surfaced.canonical_basis: magic.wizards.com: The Legends of Aetherdrift, Hour of Restoration article; Basri Ket M21 cross-set anchor.

alternative_considered_and_refused.alternative: Branch-attach all three to a new generic 'aetherdrift-grand-prix-teams' faction-node
alternative_considered_and_refused.refusal: That would be a meta-node abstracting over teams that don't share narrative identity — Goblin Rocketeers and Keelhaulers and Champions of Amonkhet are explicit RIVALS in the same Grand Prix, not allies. Abstracting them into one node would erase the canonical inter-team friction that's the WHOLE point of the set. Refused.

alternative_considered_and_refused.alternative: Stretch one of the three cards to attach to aether-rangers or endriders via 'opposing team in same Grand Prix' edge
alternative_considered_and_refused.refusal: That's an opposition-tag, not an edge. The Grand Prix is the SET, not a load-bearing narrative connection between two specific cards. Tag-bridge masquerading as edge. Refused.

notes: Refusal posture is the right call here. The three trivia-attested team-cohort flags are real signals — Wizards designed them in deliberately — but each team is below threshold-of-3 in our corpus. Park as triage entries; commission when the corpus density grows. The aether-rangers/endriders sibling precedent (both 3+ anchors at commissioning time) is the correct standard to hold to.
